title: Can Patients Have Semi-Permanent Makeup After A Treatment？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Can Patients Have Semi-Permanent Makeup After A Treatment？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So can you have semi-permanent makeup after aesthetics treatments [Music] well the honest answer to this is there will be no data no one has ever run an experiment on this to find out why so we tend to just use some fundamental principles on all of these treatments that might occ…

segments.text: so can you have semi-permanent makeup after aesthetics treatments [Music] well the honest answer to this is there will be no data no one has ever run an experiment on this to find out why so we tend to just use some fundamental principles on all of these treatments that might occur around about the same time as an aesthetic injectable treatment and that's essentially to leave at least a two week gap or until all side effects or symptoms of that original treatment are gone so in the case of semi-permanent makeup you might get a little bit of redness or inflammation you might get a little scab you might get an outbreak of cold sores if you're unlucky and you've done something around the lips and all of that should have settled down before you do any injectable treatments and that's really just to keep things simple so if you have any side effects complications you're not trying to figure out what the cause is you've left enough of a difference in time and difference in terms of when the treatment is done that you can say anything you're experiencing you can say anything you're experiencing is likely due to my treatment rather than something else
than something else and that's what my advice would be is a minimum of two weeks or until all side effects of the other treatment are completely gone you

transcript: so can you have semi-permanent makeup after aesthetics treatments [Music] well the honest answer to this is there will be no data no one has ever run an experiment on this to find out why so we tend to just use some fundamental principles on all of these treatments that might occur around about the same time as an aesthetic injectable treatment and that's essentially to leave at least a two week gap or until all side effects or symptoms of that original treatment are gone so in the case of semi-permanent makeup you might get a little bit of redness or inflammation you might get a little scab you might get an outbreak of cold sores if you're unlucky and you've done something around the lips and all of that should have settled down before you do any injectable treatments and that's really just to keep things simple so if you have any side effects complications you're not trying to figure out what the cause is you've left enough of a difference in time and difference in terms of when the treatment is done that you can say anything you're experiencing you can say anything you're experiencing is likely due to my treatment rather than something else than something else and that's what my advice would be is a minimum of two weeks or until all side effects of the other treatment are completely gone you
